arguments.content:
# AI Workflow Automation — Operational Playbook
## Legal/Consulting Verticals | Phase 3: Operational Framework

**Version:** 1.0  
**Last Updated:** 2026-03-10  
**Classification:** Internal — Launch Phase

---

## 1. Deployment Procedures

### 1.1 Pre-Deployment Checklist

| Phase | Task | Owner | Verified |
|-------|------|-------|----------|
| Security | SSL/TLS certificates validated | DevOps | ☐ |
| Security | API keys rotated for production | Security | ☐ |
| Security | Data classification tags applied | Compliance | ☐ |
| Infrastructure | Load balancer health checks pass | DevOps | ☐ |
| Infrastructure | Database backups confirmed | DBA | ☐ |
| AI Models | Production model endpoints verified | ML Eng | ☐ |
| AI Models | Fallback model warm standby confirmed | ML Eng | ☐ |
| Integration | Third-party API rate limits checked | Integrations | ☐ |
| Monitoring | Alert thresholds configured | SRE | ☐ |
| Documentation | Runbooks linked in monitoring | Tech Writing | ☐ |

### 1.2 Deployment Windows

**Standard Deployments:** Tuesday–Thursday, 02:00–06:00 local time  
**Emergency Deployments:** On-call approval required (see Escalation Matrix)

### 1.3 Deployment Workflow

```
[1] Feature Complete → [2] Staging Deploy → [3] Automated Tests → [4] Security Scan]
        ↓                      ↓                    ↓                  ↓
   [5] UAT Sign-off → [6] Canary (5%) → [7] Monitor (30m) → [8] Full Rollout]
        ↓                      ↓                    ↓                  ↓
   [9] Post-deploy Validation → [10] War Room Stand-down → [11] Retro Scheduled]
```

### 1.4 Rollback Triggers

Automated rollback initiated if ANY of the following thresholds breached:

- Error rate > 1% for 5 consecutive minutes
- P95 latency > 2000ms for 10 minutes
- Authentication failure rate > 0.5%
- Core workflow success rate < 99%
- Any P1 alert fires during deployment window

### 1.5 Environment Matrix

| Environment | Purpose | Data Classification | Refresh Frequency |
|-------------|---------|---------------------|-------------------|
| Development | Feature dev | Synthetic only | Nightly |
| Staging | Pre-prod validation | Anonymized prod subset | Weekly |
| Canary | Production subset | Live (5% traffic) | Real-time |
| Production | Live workload | Live, encrypted at rest | N/A |
| DR | Failover | Async replica | Continuous |

---

## 2. Monitoring Framework

### 2.1 SLIs & SLOs

| Service Level Indicator | Target | Measurement Window | Alert Threshold |
|-------------------------|--------|-------------------|---------------|
| Workflow Completion Rate | 99.9% | Rolling 30 days | < 99.5% |
| Document Processing Time (P50) | < 30s | Per workflow type | > 60s |
| Document Processing Time (P99) | < 120s | Per workflow type | > 300s |
| API Availability | 99.99% | Rolling 30 days | < 99.95% |
| AI Model Accuracy | > 95% | Weekly sample review | < 90% |
| Mean Time to Resolution (MTTR) | < 4 hours | Per incident severity | > 8 hours |

### 2.2 Dashboard Hierarchy

**Executive Dashboard** (C-suite view)
- Overall system health (RAG status)
- Active customer count
- Revenue-impacting incidents
- Compliance posture

**Operations Dashboard** (NOC/SRE view)
- Real-time throughput metrics
- Error rates by service
- Queue depths and processing lag
- Infrastructure resource utilization

**AI/ML Dashboard** (Data Science view)
- Model drift metrics
- Prediction confidence distributions
- A/B test results
- Hallucination rate tracking

**Customer Success Dashboard** (CS view)
- Per-customer health scores
- Onboarding milestone tracker
- Support ticket volume by tier
- Feature adoption rates

### 2.3 Alert Severity Definitions

| Severity | Response Time | Escalation Path | Example |
|----------|---------------|-----------------|---------|
| P1 — Critical | 15 minutes | Page on-call → Manager → VP Eng | Complete service outage |
| P2 — High | 30 minutes | Slack alert → On-call → Manager | Degraded performance > 50% users |
| P3 — Medium | 4 hours | Ticket queue | Isolated feature failure |
| P4 — Low | Next business day | Backlog | Cosmetic issues, minor bugs |

### 2.4 Key Health Metrics

```yaml
Critical Alarms:
  - name: ai_workflow_thoroughput_drop
    condition: workflows_per_minute < baseline * 0.5
    duration: 5m
    auto_page: true
    
  - name: document_processing_backlog
    condition: queue_depth > 10000
    duration: 10m
    auto_page: true
    
  - name: model_confidence_degradation
    condition: avg_confidence < 0.85
    duration: 15m
    auto_page: false
    
  - name: customer_facing_error_spike
    condition: error_rate_5xx > 0.01
    duration: 3m
    auto_page: true
```

---

## 3. Incident Response

### 3.1 Incident Commander Rotation

| Timezone (UTC) | Primary | Secondary |
|----------------|---------|-----------|
| 00:00–08:00 | APAC Team Lead | Senior SRE |
| 08:00–16:00 | EMEA Team Lead | Platform Engineer |
| 16:00–00:00 | Americas Team Lead | DevOps Engineer |

### 3.2 Incident Response Lifecycle

**Detection** (T+0)
- Alert fires or manual detection
- Incident Commander (IC) paged (P1/P2) or ticket created (P3/P4)
- War room bridge established (P1/P2)

**Triage** (T+15 min for P1)
- Severity confirmed and documented
- Customer impact assessed
- Regulatory notification requirements evaluated
- Runbook initiated if exists

**Mitigation** (T+30 min for P1)
- Immediate customer impact stopped (hotfix, rollback, or bypass)
- Root cause investigation begins in parallel
- Status page updated (for customer-impacting incidents)

**Resolution** (variable)
- Permanent fix deployed and validated
- Service restored to established baselines
- All war room participants confirm stand-down

**Post-Incident** (T+24–72 hours)
- Post-mortem document drafted
- Action items assigned with owners and SLAs
- Customer communications sent (if applicable)
- Lessons learned shared via monthly incident review

### 3.3 Communication Templates

**Internal Status Update (Slack)**
```
:alert: INCIDENT-YYYY-MM-DD-### — [BRIEF DESCRIPTION]
Severity: [P1/P2/P3/P4]
Started: [TIMESTAMP UTC]
Impact: [CUSTOMER IMPACT DESCRIPTION]
Status: [INVESTIGATING/MITIGATED/RESOLVED]
IC: [NAME]
Comms: [LINK TO STATUS PAGE/BRIDGE]
```

**Customer-Facing Status Page Update**
```
[OUTAGE/DEGRADATION/MAINTENANCE] — [SERVICE NAME]
We are currently investigating reports of [ISSUE].
Affected: [SCOPE — e.g., "Users in North America region"]
Started: [TIME UTC]
Update 1: [Investigation underway, identified...]
Update 2: [Mitigation deployed, monitoring...]
Resolved: [Root cause summary and recurrence prevention statement]
```

**Executive Summary (Email)**
- Sent within 2 hours of P1 resolution
- Includes: timeline, impact assessment (customers, revenue, data), immediate actions taken, and next steps

### 3.4 Regulatory Incident Triggers

| Trigger Event | Notification Required | Timeline | Recipient |
|-------------|----------------------|----------|-----------|
| Unauthorized data access | Data breach notification | 72 hours | Supervisory authority + affected customers |
| AI output causes material legal harm | Professional liability event | 24 hours | Legal counsel + insurance carrier |
| System unavailability > 4 hours | SLA breach | Immediate (per contract) | Affected enterprise customers |
| Document retention failure | Compliance violation | Immediate | Compliance officer + legal |

---

## 4. Runbook Library

### 4.1 Runbook: AI Workflow Performance Degradation

**Symptom:** Workflows completing slowly or timing out

1. Check current queue depth: `kubectl get queues`
2. Verify model endpoint health: `curl /health on inference service`
3. If rate-limiting detected, temporarily scale inference replicas: `kubectl scale deployment inference --replicas=6`
4.

# AI Workflow Automation — Customer Onboarding Templates
## Legal/Consulting Verticals | Phase 3: Operational Framework

**Version:** 1.0  
**Last Updated:** 2026-03-10  

---

## Template A: Initial Discovery Session

### Session Overview
**Purpose:** Understand customer workflows, pain points, and success criteria  
**Duration:** 60 minutes  
**Attendees:** Customer Success Manager, Solution Architect, Customer stakeholder(s)

### Agenda

| Time | Topic | Owner | Output |
|------|-------|-------|--------|
| 0:00–0:05 | Welcome & introductions | CSM | Rapport established |
| 0:05–0:15 | Current state assessment | CSM | Documented workflow map |
| 0:15–0:30 | Pain point deep-dive | Solution Architect | Prioritized pain list |
| 0:30–0:45 | AI capability preview | Solution Architect | Feasibility assessment |
| 0:45–0:55 | Success criteria definition | CSM | Signed-off KPIs |
| 0:55–1:00 | Next steps agreement | CSM | Action items & timeline |

### Discovery Questions

**Workflow Understanding**
- Walk me through a typical case/file from intake to completion
- Which steps consume the most time today?
- Where do errors or delays most commonly occur?
- What documents/data do you currently process manually?
- Are there seasonal or cyclical volume patterns?

**Current Tools**
- What systems do you use for document management today?
- How do you currently handle versions and approvals?
- What's your current approach to data security and access control?
- Do you have any existing API integrations or automation?

**Pain Points**
- What would you automate today if you could?
- What frustrates your team most about current processes?
- What happens when something falls through the cracks?
- Have you had any compliance or audit findings related to workflow management?

**Success Definition**
- What would "success" look like in 90 days?
- How would we measure improved efficiency?
- What concerns do stakeholders have about AI assistance?
- Who needs to approve new technology adoption?

### Output Document: Discovery Summary

**Customer Name:** _______________  
**Date:** _______________  
**CSM:** _______________

**Workflow Summary:**
*(Diagram or bulleted steps)*

**Key Pain Points (Prioritized):**
1. 
2. 
3. 

**Primary Use Cases Identified:**
- 
- 
- 

**Success Criteria (KPIs):**
| Metric | Baseline | Target | Measurement Method |
|--------|----------|--------|-------------------|
| | | | |
| | | | |

**Risks & Concerns:**
- 
- 

**Next Steps:**
| Action | Owner | Due Date |
|--------|-------|----------|
| | | |

---

## Template B: Technical Implementation Plan

### Document Overview
**Purpose:** Detail technical requirements and implementation timeline  
**Audience:** Customer IT, Security, Legal/Compliance

### Section 1: System Architecture

```
[CUSTOMER ENVIRONMENT]                     [AI WORKFLOW PLATFORM]
+------------------+                       +----------------------+
| Document Store   |←-- Integration API --+| Ingestion Pipeline   |
| (SharePoint/     |                       +----------------------+
|  NetDocuments/   |                               ↓
|  iManage)        |                       +----------------------+
+------------------+                       | AI Processing Engine |
        ↓                                  +----------------------+
+------------------+                               ↓
| Matter/Case      |                       +----------------------+
| Management       ←---- Webhooks ---------| Output & Actions     |
+------------------+                       +----------------------+
```

### Section 2: Integration Checklist

**Phase 1: Pre-Integration (Week 1)**
- [ ] API credentials provisioned
- [ ] IP allowlist configured (if required)
- [ ] SSL/TLS certificate validation
- [ ] Data classification agreement signed
- [ ] Staging environment access granted

**Phase 2: Document Store Integration (Week 2)**
- [ ] Connector configured for primary DMS
- [ ] Metadata mapping defined
- [ ] Access control synchronization tested
- [ ] Document version handling validated
- [ ] Retention policy alignment confirmed

**Phase 3: Workflow Configuration (Week 3)**
- [ ] Use case-specific workflows built
- [ ] Validation rules configured
- [ ] Human-in-the-loop checkpoints defined
- [ ] Exception handling routes mapped
- [ ] Escalation thresholds set

**Phase 4: Testing & Validation (Week 4)**
- [ ] Synthetic data test cases executed
- [ ] Customer sample documents processed
- [ ] Output accuracy validated (≥95% target)
- [ ] Edge cases documented and handled
- [ ] Performance benchmarks met

**Phase 5: Production Deployment (Week 5)**
- [ ] Production credentials provisioned
- [ ] Monitoring dashboards configured
- [ ] Go-live checklist completed
- [ ] Training sessions delivered
- [ ]

# AI Workflow Automation — Team Structure & Role Definitions
## Legal/Consulting Verticals | Phase 3: Operational Framework

**Version:** 1.0  
**Last Updated:** 2026-03-10  
**Applies to:** Launch Phase (Months 1–6)

---

## 1. Organizational Structure

```
                         ┌─────────────────────────────────┐
                         │           CEO/Founder           │
                         └──────────────┬──────────────────┘
                                        │
                    ┌───────────────────┼───────────────────┐
                    │                   │                   │
        ┌───────────▼────────┐ ┌────────▼────────┐ ┌───────▼────────┐
        │   VP Engineering   │ │  VP Customer    │ │   VP Product   │
        │                    │ │    Success      │ │   & Strategy   │
        └───────────┬────────┘ └────────┬────────┘ └───────┬────────┘
                    │                   │                   │
    ┌───────────────┼───────┐   ┌───────┼───────┐   ┌──────┴────────┐
    │               │       │   │       │       │   │               │
┌───▼──┐     ┌─────▼────┐ ┌▼───┴─┐ ┌───▼───┐ ┌─▼──┴┐         ┌─────▼─────┐
│Platform│    │  AI/ML   │ │Integr│ │Support│ │CSM  │         │ Vertical  │
│  Team  │    │  Team    │ │ations│ │ Team  │ │Team │         │  Experts  │
└────────┘    └──────────┘ └──────┘ └───────┘ └─────┘         └───────────┘
```

---

## 2. Leadership Roles

### CEO/Founder
**Reports to:** Board of Directors  
**Direct Reports:** VP Engineering, VP Customer Success, VP Product & Strategy

**Core Responsibilities:**
- Strategic direction and vision
- Investor relations and fundraising
- Culture and values champion
- Executive customer relationships
- Final escalation point

**Key Metrics:**
- ARR growth rate
- Customer retention (NRR)
- Employee satisfaction
- Time to close strategic deals

### VP Engineering
**Reports to:** CEO  
**Direct Reports:** Platform Team Lead, AI/ML Team Lead, Integrations Team Lead, QA Lead

**Core Responsibilities:**
- Product delivery and reliability
- Technical architecture decisions
- Engineering culture and process
- Security and compliance posture
- Budget and resource allocation

**Key Metrics:**
- System uptime (99.99% target)
- Deployment frequency
- Change failure rate
- Mean time to recovery (MTTR)
- Engineering velocity

**Launch Phase Priorities:**
- Stabilize production systems
- Achieve SOC 2 Type II certification
- Build AI/ML operational capabilities
- Establish 24/7 on-call coverage

### VP Customer Success
**Reports to:** CEO  
**Direct Reports:** Support Manager, CSM Manager, Implementation Manager

**Core Responsibilities:**
- Customer retention and expansion
- Support and implementation quality
- Customer health monitoring
- Professional services offerings
- Voice of customer into product

**Key Metrics:**
- Net Revenue Retention (NRR > 120%)
- Gross Revenue Retention (GRR > 90%)
- Net Promoter Score (NPS > 40)
- Time to value (TTV < 30 days)
- Support CSAT (> 4.5/5)

**Launch Phase Priorities:**
- Onboard first 10 customers successfully
- Establish repeatable onboarding playbook
- Build support organization to handle SLA commitments
- Identify and cultivate customer references

### VP Product & Strategy
**Reports to:** CEO  
**Direct Reports:** Vertical Experts, Competitive Intelligence, Pricing & Packaging

**Core Responsibilities:**
- Product roadmap and prioritization
- Market and competitive intelligence
- Vertical strategy (Legal, Consulting, etc.)
- Pricing and packaging
- Strategic partnerships

**Key Metrics:**
- Feature adoption rates
- Time to market for new features
- Win/loss rate
- Deal velocity
- Product-market fit score

**Launch Phase Priorities:**
- Legal/Consulting vertical deep-dive
- Feature prioritization for v1.x
- Partnership strategy with DMS providers
- Reference customer program

---

## 3. Operational Teams

### 3.1 Platform Engineering Team
**Lead:** Platform Team Lead  
**Team Size (Launch):** 4 engineers  
**Location:** Distributed / Hybrid

**Roles:**
| Role | Count | Key Skills |
|------|-------|------------|
| Senior Backend Engineer | 2 | Node.js/Python, microservices, Kafka |
| DevOps / SRE | 1 | Kubernetes, Terraform, observability |
| Security Engineer | 1 | Application security, compliance, pentesting |

**Responsibilities:**
- Core platform infrastructure
- API development and maintenance
- Database design and optimization
- Security implementation and audits
- Observability and monitoring
- On-call rotation (24/7)

**Success Metrics:**
- API response time P99 < 200ms
- Zero security incidents
- Deployment frequency > 1/day
- 80% alert coverage

###

# AI Workflow Automation — Support Escalation Procedures
## Legal/Consulting Verticals | Phase 3: Operational Framework

**Version:** 1.0  
**Last Updated:** 2026-03-10  
**Classification:** Internal — Support Team Use

---

## 1. Support Structure Overview

### 1.1 Tier Definitions

| Tier | Scope | Response SLA | Team |
|------|-------|--------------|------|
| **L1 — Help Desk** | Password resets, access issues, basic how-to questions | 4 hours | Customer Support Associates |
| **L2 — Technical Support** | Workflow configuration, integration troubleshooting, bug triage | 8 hours | Technical Support Engineers |
| **L3 — Engineering Escalation** | Root cause analysis, hotfix development, complex integrations | 24 hours | Platform/AI/Integrations Teams |
| **L4 — Executive Escalation** | Contract issues, data incidents, regulatory concerns | 4 hours | Directors + Legal |

### 1.2 Coverage Hours

| Tier | Weekdays | Weekends | Holidays |
|------|----------|----------|----------|
| L1 | 06:00–22:00 local | 10:00–18:00 | On-call for P1 only |
| L2 | 06:00–22:00 local | On-call | On-call |
| L3 | Business hours | P1 only | P1 only |
| L4 | Business hours | P1/P2 | P1/P2 |

---

## 2. Escalation Matrix

### 2.1 Technical Escalations

| Issue Type | L1 Handles? | L2 Handles? | L3 Required? | Notes |
|------------|-----------|-------------|--------------|-------|
| Password reset | ✅ | — | — | Self-service preferred |
| User access provisioning | ✅ | — | — | Standard approval workflow |
| Document upload failures | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | Check file size/format first |
| Workflow not triggering | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | Check configuration |
| Output accuracy degradation | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | Review model version |
| API integration failure | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | Check credentials/expiry |
| Data sync issues | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | Verify connector health |
| Complete service outage | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | Immediate P1 escalation |
| AI model hallucination | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | Log sample, route to ML |
| Security incident | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | Immediate security team |
| Performance degradation | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | Capture metrics, route to platform |
| Custom integration development | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | Professional services |

### 2.2 Business Escalations

| Issue Type | Route To | Timeline | Requires |
|------------|----------|----------|----------|
| Contract/proposal questions | Sales/Account Exec | 24 hours | Customer context |
| Billing disputes | Finance + CSM | 48 hours | Invoice review |
| SLA breach | VP Customer Success | Immediate | Incident commander |
| Data subject request | Compliance Officer | 72 hours (GDPR) | Legal review |
| Regulatory inquiry | Legal + VP Eng | 24 hours | Documentation |
| Customer threatening churn | CSM + VP CS | 4 hours | Executive sponsor |
| Negative PR/social | Marketing + Legal | Immediate | Statement prep |

---

## 3. Escalation Workflows

### 3.1 Standard Escalation Path

```
[Customer] → [L1 Support] → [L2 Technical] → [L3 Engineering] → [L4 Executive]
              ↓                  ↓                  ↓                  ↓
           4hr SLA          8hr SLA           24hr SLA         Case-by-case

Rules:
- L1 must attempt resolution before escalation
- Escalation requires documented attempt history
- L2 can engage L3 via Slack #escalations channel
- L3 involvement = war room for P1/P2
- L4 only for contract/legal/customer-facing issues
```

### 3.2 Severity-Based Escalation

**Critical (P1)** — Service Unavailable
```
T+0: Alert fires or customer reports
T+5m: L1 validates issue, opens incident
T+10m: Incident Commander assigned (rotating L3 engineer)
T+15m: War room established, customer communication sent
T+30m: L3 full engagement, rollback if needed
T+60m: VP Engineering notified
T+4h: Executive briefing if unresolved
T+resolution: Post-mortem scheduled within 24h
```

**High (P2)** — Service Degraded
```
T+0: Issue detected
T+30m: L2 attempts resolution
T+2h: If unresolved, L3 engaged via ticket escalation
T+4h: VP Customer Success looped in if customer-impacting
T+24h: For unresolved P2, automatically promoted to P1
```

**Medium (P3)** — Feature Issue / Non-Critical
```
Standard queue processing
Escalated to L2 if L1 cannot resolve within 2 attempts
L3 engaged only for pattern detection (multiple similar issues)
Goal: resolution within 5 business days
```

**Low (P4)** — Enhancement Request / Minor Issue
```
Backlog queue
Monthly review for prioritization
Direct mapping to product roadmap when accepted
```

### 3.3 Emergency Escalation (Skip Levels)

**When This Applies:**
- Active data breach suspected
- Customer confidential data exposed
- Regulatory deadline at risk
- Complete system unavailability
- Customer legal action threatened

**Procedure:**
1. Caller states: "This is an emergency escalation — [brief description]"
2. Skip directly to level with authority to resolve
3. Notify chain simultaneously (not sequentially)
4. Document decision to escalate

**Authority Levels:**
| Level | Authority | Contact Method |
|-------|-----------|----------------|
| 1 | Pause services | #incidents Slack |
| 2 | Rollback deployment | PagerDuty |
| 3 | Invoke DR site | Phone + Slack |
| 4 | Customer communication | Executive chain |
| 5 | Legal/regulatory | CEO + Legal team |

---

## 4. Support Case Lifecycle

### 4.1 Case Creation

**Required Fields:**
- Customer name & contract tier
- Contact information
- Severity classification (P1–P4)
- Product area (AI/Integration/Platform)
- Reproducibility (always/sometimes/cannot reproduce)
- Impact scope (user count, workflows affected)
- Initial description & attachments

**Automatic Routing Rules:**
```
IF severity = P1 → Immediate alert + assign to on-call L3
IF product_area = AI → Route to ML Support queue
IF product_area = Integration → Route to Integration queue
IF customer_tier = Enterprise → CSM notified automatically
IF issue_type = security → Security team copied
```

### 4.2 Case Updates

**Update Frequency:**
| Severity | Customer Update | Internal Update |
|----------|-----------------|-----------------|
| P1 | Every 30 minutes | Real-time in war room |
| P2 | Every 4 hours | Every 2 hours |
| P3 | Daily | Every 24 hours |
| P4 | Per sprint/when resolved | Weekly summary |

**Update Template:**
```
Subject: [P1/P2/P3] — [Company] — [Brief Issue Summary] — [Status]

Current Status: [INVESTIGATING/MITIGATED/RESOLVED]
Next Action: [What we're doing now + owner]
ETA: [When next update or resolution expected]
Workaround: [If available]
Impact: [Current customer impact]
```

### 4.3 Case Closure

**Resolution Criteria:**
- Issue verified fixed by customer
- 72 hours without response from customer (with follow-up)
- Resolved by workaround customer accepts
- Converted to feature request (with approval)
- Duplicate of existing case (linked)

**Required Closing Actions:**
- [ ] Resolution documented in case
- [ ] Root cause noted (if known)
- [ ] Knowledge base article created (if new issue type)
- [ ] Customer satisfaction survey sent (P1/P2)
- [ ] Related cases linked
- [ ] JIRA ticket created for product improvement (if applicable)

---

## 5. Customer Communication

### 5.1 Status Page Integration

**Automated Posting Triggers:**
| Event | Status | Audience |
|-------|--------|----------|
| P1 incident declared | Investigating | Public |
| P1 mitigated | Monitoring | Public |
| P1 resolved | Resolved | Public + post-mortem link |
| P2 affecting >10% customers | Partial Outage | Subscribers |
| Scheduled maintenance | Maintenance | Public (24hr advance notice) |

**Manual Posting Required For:**
- Security incidents (coordination with Legal)
- Data integrity issues
- Regulatory-mandated notifications
- Customer-specific issues (not posted publicly)

### 5.2 Customer Notification Templates
